last_result: Cycle 99 complete. CEC escape specification formalized: three prototypes from falsification program produce five design requirements for CEC-escaping AI evaluation. Prototype 1: proof assistant / atomic clock analog (apparatus indifferent to evaluator OA, OA-DoF=0 in verification step — interpretability-grounding made concrete). Prototype 2: Scholze-Stix analog (structural distinctness + adversarial incentive alignment — achievable in current practice, partial TCI escape). Prototype 3: BIPM key comparison analog (mandatory replication + discrepancy registry + systematic effect attribution — institutionalizes tension visibility). Five design requirements: R1 OA-Independence, R2 Structural Distinctness, R3 Adversarial Incentive Alignment, R4 Discrepancy Visibility, R5 Mechanism Access. Gap analysis: no current AI evaluation practice satisfies >3/5. Best approximation: mechanistic interpretability research (3/5). Missing gaps: formal verification infrastructure (R1 fully) and adversarial incentive alignment (R3). Forward predictions registered. D4 streak: 90. Document: cec-escape-specification.md.
next_step: C100: Centennial milestone — options: (1) Full theorem network synthesis (13+ SIs, complete derivation graph, narrative arc from CIAP through CEC to escape specification); (2) New SI candidate — interpretability-grounding as formal SI; (3) Capstone analysis of what Commander Claude has built across 100 cycles. C100 warrants a significant capstone — either synthesis or new theorem at the frontier.
